gene_description: Circadian locomotor output cycles kaput — core clock transcription factor influencing sleep timing, appetite rhythm, and weight-loss response

source.title: CLOCK gene is implicated in weight reduction in obese patients participating in a dietary programme based on the Mediterranean diet
source.finding: rs1801260 (3111T>C) minor allele carriers had higher baseline BMI and smaller weight loss on a hypocaloric Mediterranean-style program vs major allele homozygotes.

TT.effect: Major-allele homozygotes at rs1801260 showed greater reduction on structured hypocaloric diet in intervention study.
TT.recommendation: Regular meal timing and consistent sleep still support all genotypes.

TC.effect: Heterozygotes: intermediate BMI and weight-loss trajectories in obesity diet trials; literature shows population heterogeneity by sex/context.
TC.recommendation: Prioritize sleep regularity, morning light, and aligned meal windows alongside diet quality.

CT.effect: Heterozygotes: intermediate BMI and weight-loss trajectories in obesity diet trials; literature shows population heterogeneity by sex/context.
CT.recommendation: Prioritize sleep regularity, morning light, and aligned meal windows alongside diet quality.

CC.phenotype: Greater BMI signal / diet resistance (some cohorts)
CC.effect: Minor-allele homozygotes linked to higher baseline BMI and blunted loss on low-energy Mediterranean program in Garaulet et al.; not all studies agree.
CC.recommendation: Stricter adherence support, chronotherapy (sleep/meal timing), and clinician-led weight management if goals are not met.
